title: How to maximise the Back Office order page
bodyHtml:
The Back Office order page collects all the information and tools you need to process orders in one place. Learn how to maximise your use of the page for quicker, better results. 

 

Jump to
How do I find the order page?
All you need to know about the order page
All you need to know about looking at individual orders
How do I process or modify an order?

 

How do I find the order page?

After logging into the Back office, select “Orders” in the menu bar to get to the orders page. 

 

All you need to know about the order page

The order page is divided into 4 main sections: 

  • All orders
  • New orders 
  • Pending orders
  • Orders with open tasks



Using filters

Once you’ve got orders coming in, you’ll be able to find information using a number of filters. Here’s a breakdown of the filters and where you can find them

 

Name

Function

Can be found in

Order number

Filter by order number

All pages

Start date

Filter by date (beginning)

All pages

End date

Filter by date (end)

All pages

BackShip

Filter by BackShip orders/not/both

All pages

SKU

Filter by SKU

All pages

Customer first name

Filter by customer first name

All pages

Customer first name

Filter by customer last name

All pages

Market

Customer

All pages

Order status

Filter by order status

“All orders” page


 

Importing and exporting

You can always import and export information from the orders page. The results of your imports/exports will be available in the file library page. 

 

Here’s an overview of the information you can export: 

 

Name

Action associated

Export all orders

Export all orders within a chosen time frame.

Export missing IMEIs

Export all orders for which you still need to register an IMEI number.

Export packing slips

Export all packing slips for selected orders within a chosen time frame.

Export BackShip label if BackShip seller

Export all of your BackShip labels within a chosen time frame.

 

When exporting, you’ll have the option to limit or filter the information you export by things like date, language, order status, and more. 

 

Here’s an overview of the information you can import:

 

Name

Action associated

Import tracking number

Import tracking numbers for specific orders in bulk.

Import IMEIs

Import IMEIs for specific orders in bulk.

 

Keep in mind that if you’ve applied a filter to your orders page, the filter will still apply when you go to import or export information. 

 

Understanding order statuses

Your orders will be labelled with a status that reflects its progress. When your order moves from one status to another, it will include different sets of relevant information about the order.

When your order is “To be processed,” it will look like this:  

 

Here’s a breakdown of the information that you’ll see when your order is “To be processed.”

Name

Meaning

Action associated

Order ID

The identification number of the specific order

Will redirect you to a more detailed order page when selected

Name

Name of the customer for this order

None

Country

The country/market in which the order was placed

None

Date

Exact date and time that the order was placed

None

Shipping provider

Shipping method chosen by the customer

None

Shipping price

What the customer paid for their shipping method

None

Shipping ETA

Reminder of when the order has to be shipped (according to the Quality Charter)

None

Order line item ID

The ID number of a specific order line item

None

SKU

The unique code for the product sold

None

Quantity

The quantity of products ordered

None

Price

Total price of the order

None

Payment method

Which payment method the customer used

None

Status

The order status — changes based on where the order stands in processing

None

“Confirm” button

Select this button to confirm your intend to process and ship this order

Will change the order status from “To be processed” to “To be shipped”

 

Once the order status is “To be shipped” it should look like this:

 

 

Here’s a quick outline of the new information you’ll see on the order page.

 

Name

Meaning

Action associated

“Print packing slip” button

This button is used open and download your packing slip

Will redirect you to the packing slip in a PDF

“Ship” button

This button is used to indicate you’re shipping a specific order

Will redirect you to the order shipping page

 

Once the order status is “Shipped”, nothing new will appear. 

 

Updating multiple orders

You can update more than 1 order by selecting the empty square above the individual order listings. You can also choose your orders by batch by selecting the empty squares next to each individual order.

 

Once the orders are selected, you can:

  • Confirm selected orders
  • Print packing slips
  • Create BackShip labels (if you’re a BackShip seller)



 

All you need to know about looking at individual orders

When you select an order ID, you’ll be redirected to a page with detailed information about the order in question. 

 

Here’s what you’ll find on the page:

 

Name

Meaning

Action associated

“Contact” button

This button allows you to contact the customer service agent assigned to this order

Will redirect you to the correct customer service page

Order status

Shows status changes and dates

None

Order details

Shows all order details including product name, grade, etc.

None

Shipping details

Shows all shipping details like shipping date, shipping provider, and tracking number

None

Orderline details

Shows all orderline details like orderline ID, SKU, product, IMEI, and price

None

Shipping address

Shows the customer shipping address and contact details

None

Billing Address

Shows the customer billing address

None



 

How do I process or modify an order?

As you progress through the process of completing an order, you’ll be able to select buttons to indicate you’re ready to move on to the next step. These buttons give you the ability to confirm the order, indicate you’re ready to ship the order, or cancel the order.  
 


Confirming an order

To confirm an order, go to the “New orders” page and select “Confirm”. You can also select multiple orders and confirm them all by using the “Confirm selected orders” button.

Now, you’ll be able to ship the order and add an IMEI number to the device. To do so, simply select “Ship.”

 

After indicating you’re ready to ship the order, you’ll see the following screen:

 

 

Here’s a brief explanation of each field:

 

Name

Action associated

Shipping provider

You can choose between all the different shipping providers available on our platform. If you’re a BackShip seller, you’ll find all BackShip options at the top of the list.

Tracking number

You can enter the tracking number of your parcel.

IMEI

You can enter the IMEI number for the device you’re shipping.

“Ship items” button

Once all the information has been entered, you can select this button to ship the order.

 

Once you’ve progressed past this screen, you’ll need to ship your order. You’ll still have the option to contact a Back Market agent by selecting the three dots and then “Contact agent”. 

 

Cancelling an order before it’s been shipped

If you need to cancel an order, here’s how to do it. Go to the “New orders” page and select the 3 dots. Then, select “Cancel order”. 

 

Refunding an order after it’s been shipped

There are 2 different scenarios when it comes to refunding an order — depending on if you’ve refunded the order before or after it’s been shipped. 

 

In order to make a refund before the order has been shipped, select the 3 dots next to the order > “Refund this order”. You’ll be able to choose the amount you want to refund. 

 

To refund an order that’s already been shipped, you’ll need to contact Back Market customer service. Select the 3 dots next to the order > "Contact agent" to open a request.



 

If you found this article clear and helpful, hit the like button to let us know.
